The question is asking for the sum of square miles from both Sandy Bay and Longwood districts. Since each row represents a distinct district and the "area\nsq mi" column contains the square mile measurement for each, we can directly add the values from these two districts without needing to group. We can use the f_sum(area\nsq mi) operation to calculate the total square miles combined from both districts.
The area of Longwood is 12.9 square miles and the area of Sandy Bay is 5.9 square miles. By adding these areas together, the total number of square miles in Sandy Bay and Longwood is 18.8.
